title: prostanthera saxicola var. major
intro: Prostanthera saxicola var. major, also known as the rock lavender or major variegated mintbush, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds such as linalool and borneol. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its calming and anti-inflammatory effects. Prostanthera saxicola var. major can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
